title: Targeted Neuromodulation of the Left Dorsolateral Prefrontal Cortex Alleviates Altered Affective Response Evaluation in Lonely Individuals
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings and behavioral data from 120 neurologically healthy adults who completed an emotion processing and regulation task during transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS). Participants underwent two counterbalanced sessions (sham and active stimulation) while viewing static emotional images and performing either passive viewing or cognitive reappraisal. Stimulation was targeted to the dorsolateral prefrontal cortex using two between-subject alternative montages: left dlPFC (anode at F3) or right dlPFC (anode at F4). The study investigates how targeted neuromodulation of the dlPFC modulates affective response evaluation in individuals experiencing loneliness. This is a BIDS-formatted dataset derived from OpenNeuro (ds006861).
methods_description: EEG was recorded from 32 active electrodes using a QuickCap and Neuroscan SynampsRT amplifier at 1000 Hz sampling rate with impedance maintained below 10 kΩ. tDCS was delivered via Starstim 8 system with anodal electrode at either F3 (left dlPFC) or F4 (right dlPFC) in a between-subjects design, with return electrodes at FP1/FP2, FZ, FC1/FC2, FC5/FC6 respectively (8 cm² anodal, π cm² return electrode areas). Active stimulation consisted of 2 mA for 20 minutes with 30-second ramp-up; sham sessions included only ramp-up periods. EEG preprocessing included 0.1–30 Hz band-pass filtering, downsampling to 250 Hz, re-referencing to average mastoids, ICA decomposition with automatic IC rejection (ADJUST and SASICA), and artifact rejection using absolute amplitude and FASTER epoch properties criteria.
